nodes.label: AI as debugging assistant
nodes.grounding_span: the purpose of AI was to spot bugs and point them out to me

nodes.label: AI as foundational structure provider
nodes.grounding_span: I didn't use AI until I had a version of the game that I could use/test

nodes.label: AI as verification-requiring tool

nodes.label: AI as resource expander for course planning
nodes.grounding_span: If AI could create a study guide with links, or that just brings all the relevant content into one place

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: I wanted to actual spend my limited time debugging, as opposed to looking for bugs

nodes.label: Skill development and learning
nodes.grounding_span: test and improve my coding skills

nodes.label: Trust in educational resources
nodes.grounding_span: I trust the courses solution enough that I don't feel the need to explore it with AI

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for debugging
nodes.grounding_span: this was useful as it made me rethink some of my logic

nodes.label: Systematic problem decomposition and incremental development
